requests.notes: Storage/migration feature — no dedicated HTTP surface. Verify by other means: save this request, restart the app, confirm it reloads cleanly (on-read migration upgrades the stored doc to the current schemaVersion). Old data with no schemaVersion is upgraded silently. Covered by src/app/store unit tests.
requests.name: Echo — round-trip a saved request

requests.notes: Keystore/crypto feature — no dedicated HTTP surface. Verify by hand: with a secret stored (the Bearer token here), simulate a keystore mismatch (move/relock the profile). The field must show an inline 'couldn't decrypt — re-enter' state (NOT blank), and the still-recoverable ciphertext must be preserved on the next save.
requests.name: Echo — request carrying a secret

requests.notes: Storage I/O feature — no dedicated HTTP surface. Verify by hand: rapidly rename/edit/save to force overlapping writes (last-write-wins, no partial files); plant a stale *.tmp file under the userData dir, restart, confirm it is GC'd while real data survives.
requests.name: Echo — drive overlapping saves

requests.notes: Workspace backup/restore is file I/O, not HTTP. Verify the round-trip: Export everything → wipe the profile → Import → collections/environments/settings match. Confirm secrets are redacted (mode 'none') or ciphertext (never plaintext) in the archive. This suite's own archives exercise the export path.
requests.name: Echo — seed data before export

requests.notes: The collection has sendCookies on. /echo reflects received cookies under `cookies`. Send a request that emits Set-Cookie first (any endpoint), then send this to confirm the jar auto-attaches the captured cookie.
requests.name: Echo — inspect attached cookies

requests.notes: Validate cookie scoping: a cookie scoped to a different path/host must NOT be attached here — /echo will show it absent.
requests.name: Echo — path/domain scoping

requests.notes: Validates first-class API-Key auth placement; mock echoes the parsed key.
requests.name: API key (header)

requests.notes: Validates the 401 WWW-Authenticate challenge → re-send Authorization: Digest round-trip.
requests.name: Digest auth

requests.notes: Validates the multi-leg NTLM handshake.
requests.name: NTLM auth

requests.notes: Validates a large body spills to a temp file + preview, with no renderer freeze.
requests.name: Volume — large (~28MB)

requests.notes: Validates the preview / 'view full / save to file' affordance.
requests.name: Volume — medium (~15MB)

requests.notes: Control: renders inline normally. Streamed temp files should be GC'd afterwards.
requests.name: Volume — small (~1MB) control

requests.notes: Internal test-suite feature — verify via `make test` (variable-resolution, import/export, HTTP-execution tests pass), not via the collection. This request just exercises the {{var}} resolver the tests cover.
requests.name: Echo — resolved dynamic variable

requests.notes: Pure refactor (no behaviour change). Manually confirm in `make debug` that headers/params/body/auth sub-components all still work; /echo reflects them for a parity check.
requests.name: Echo — headers + params + body + auth

requests.notes: Covered by `make test` (data-store IPC mapping + request→response cycle). This request mirrors the loop a human would run by hand.
requests.name: Echo — edit → execute → render cycle

requests.notes: Validates an HTTP error surfaces in the response viewer (status + error), not silent success.
requests.name: Status 500 — server error surfaces

requests.notes: Validates a non-2xx status is shown clearly.
requests.name: Status 404 — not found

requests.notes: Validates a connection failure raises a visible error notification. The write-failure toast path (disk full / permission denied / keystore down) is not HTTP-triggerable — simulate a save failure to verify that.
requests.name: Dead port — connection failure

requests.notes: CI pipeline (build/infra only) — no runtime surface. Verify on GitHub: push/PR runs format-check + lint + `make test` and the macOS/Linux/Windows unsigned build matrix is green.
requests.name: Echo — placeholder

requests.notes: Packaging/signing (build only) — no runtime surface. Verify with `make dist` + credentials, then `codesign --verify --deep`/`spctl -a` (mac) and `signtool verify` (win); confirm unsigned `--dir` still builds when creds are absent.
requests.name: Echo — placeholder

requests.notes: Connect, send a text + a JSON frame, validate echoed frames appear timestamped; sent/received/system frames are visually distinct.
requests.name: WS echo — send text + JSON frames

requests.notes: Validates a server-push stream (1 frame/sec) renders incrementally as frames arrive.
requests.name: WS time — server push stream

requests.notes: Validates a 401 handshake rejection surfaces as a clear connection error. Disconnect must tear down cleanly (no leak).
requests.name: WS reject — 401 handshake

requests.notes: Validates SSE frames (event:/data:/id:) append live, not all at once.
requests.name: SSE events

requests.notes: Validates an OpenAI-style token stream renders incrementally and completes on [DONE].
requests.name: SSE LLM token stream

requests.notes: Validates Stop aborts a never-ending stream (req.destroy) and memory stays bounded.
requests.name: SSE infinite — Stop aborts

requests.notes: Validates chunked application/x-ndjson streams line by line (Stream toggle on).
requests.name: NDJSON chunked stream

requests.notes: Validates {query,variables,operationName} serialization + Content-Type: application/json.
requests.name: Query — user(id)

requests.notes: Validates {{var}} interpolation in both the query and variables panes.
requests.name: Query — with variables

requests.notes: Validates a mutation + enum (Role).
requests.name: Mutation — createUser (enum)

requests.notes: Validates schema introspection drives autocomplete; an introspection failure must be surfaced.
requests.name: Introspection (Fetch schema)

requests.notes: Validates inline image preview (bytes intact).
requests.name: PNG image

requests.notes: Validates PDF preview.
requests.name: PDF

requests.notes: Validates hex + ASCII dump with offsets for arbitrary binary.
requests.name: Octet-stream — hex dump

requests.notes: Validates Save-to-file produces a byte-accurate file.
requests.name: JPEG — save to file

requests.notes: The mock is plain HTTP, so no TLS handshake occurs. Configure a per-host client-cert entry for localhost:8888 in Settings and confirm it persists and the passphrase encrypts at rest / redacts on export. True mTLS + custom-CA validation needs an EXTERNAL https host with client-cert/self-signed config (manual): verify a per-request SSL-verify override toggles independently of the global setting.
requests.name: Echo — cert config persists (HTTP)

requests.notes: Validation target is the GENERATED code text, not the response. Use 'Generate code…' → cURL / fetch / Python requests / Go / HTTPie; each snippet must be valid & runnable, with resolved {{vars}}.
requests.name: Echo — GET with header (gen snippets)

requests.notes: Validates snippets reflect method/headers/body/auth. AWS-SigV4 should bail out of static generation; unresolved {{var}} should warn.
requests.name: Echo — POST JSON + Bearer (gen snippets)

requests.notes: Import is the artifact. Paste:  curl -X POST {{mockUrl}}/echo -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H 'X-Test: 1' -d '{"a":1}'  → confirm the imported request matches this one, send it, and check /echo reflects it. Also try a cURL with -u alice:alice123 and -F field=val. Import a small .har whose entries point at {{mockUrl}}/echo. Malformed input must raise a notification, not silently drop.
requests.name: Echo — target for cURL paste

requests.notes: Export interchange (file output). Export this folder to Postman v2.1 / Insomnia v4 / OpenAPI 3 / HAR and validate the files in their target tools; confirm secrets are redacted in every exporter and structure round-trips.
requests.name: Echo — POST JSON + Bearer (export source)

requests.notes: Import is the artifact. Import an OpenAPI 3 spec whose `servers` point at {{mockUrl}} and that uses components/$ref for a parameter and a requestBody, plus a schema-only operation. Send the imported ops and confirm $ref'd params/body are present (not dropped) and a synthesized example body with the correct Content-Type is sent. Remote $refs must be resolved or explicitly reported.
requests.name: Echo — target for OpenAPI import

requests.notes: Diagnostics/crash-reporting feature. Verify by hand: requests appear in the diagnostics/console log; a forced crash produces a crash report; logs redact secrets. No dedicated HTTP surface.
requests.name: Echo — generate a log entry

requests.notes: Configure Settings → proxy = {{mockProxyUrl}} (with optional creds). Validates HTTP proxy + credential handling; /echo confirms the relayed request.
requests.name: Echo — via HTTP proxy

requests.notes: Configure proxy = {{mockSocksUrl}}. Validates SOCKS5 agent selection.
requests.name: Echo — via SOCKS5 proxy

requests.notes: Through the proxy with retries enabled. X-Proxy-Error: 2:reset fails twice then succeeds — validates the retry policy succeeds within configured attempts + backoff (attempt count shown in Console).
requests.name: Echo — retry on injected fault

requests.notes: Put localhost in the proxy bypass/no-proxy list; validates the bypassed host connects directly, ignoring the proxy. Confirm proxy creds redact on export.
requests.name: Echo — bypass list

requests.notes: Validates a measurable TTFB / server-think segment in the waterfall summing to ≈ total.
requests.name: Delay 2s — measurable server-think

requests.notes: Plain HTTP fast path — validates the breakdown renders sensibly with no TLS segment. Use an external https URL to see DNS/TCP/TLS phases. Breakdown persists in history/Timeline replay.
requests.name: Echo — fast path (no TLS)

requests.notes: UI/a11y feature — no HTTP surface. Verify by hand: OS 'reduce motion' stops animation; forced-colors/high-contrast renders all controls; the tree is operable via Arrow/Home/End/type-ahead from a single tab stop; icon-only controls have accessible names; status changes announce via aria-live.
requests.name: Echo — populate the tree

requests.notes: Set the file row's path to a real local file before sending. Validates mixed file+text parts (filename/boundary/Content-Type); /echo reflects the parsed parts. File bytes are read in main (only the path crosses IPC).
requests.name: Multipart — file + text fields

requests.notes: Validates multiple file uploads. Point both rows at real files first.
requests.name: Multipart — two file fields

requests.notes: Validates :token detection + substitution into the sent URL ({{var}} also supported). /echo shows the resolved path users/42/posts/7.
requests.name: Path params — :id / :postId

requests.notes: Localization layer — no HTTP surface. Verify by hand: switching Appearance → Language (en/de/es/fr/it/ja/zh + system) re-localizes strings (incl. aria-label/title) and sets <html lang>; missing keys fall back to English. Gate is `make test-i18n`.
requests.name: Echo — placeholder

requests.notes: Favorite this request (star toggle) and confirm it appears in the Favorites tab across collections. Lists persist across restart and survive rename/move; deleted ids drop out.
requests.name: Echo — favorite me

requests.notes: Open/send it and confirm it enters the deduped, capped, newest-first Recents list. The [Requests | Favorites | Recent] tab bar appears only when a favorite exists and/or Recents is enabled.
requests.name: Echo — recents

requests.notes: Trigger 'Get Token' to run the device flow: user-code prompt + polling with authorization_pending/slow_down. The acquired token is attached to this /echo request. Keycloak: client device-code-client, users alice/alice123.
requests.name: OAuth2 device_code (Keycloak)

requests.notes: RFC 8693 token exchange. Supply a subject_token (from a prior login) and trigger 'Get Token'; validates the exchange + token caching.
requests.name: OAuth2 token_exchange (Keycloak)

requests.notes: Validates a correct Authorization: OAuth … signature (signed in the main process); mock /auth/oauth1 verifies + echoes it.
requests.name: OAuth 1.0a (mock)

requests.notes: Sanity check that an existing grant didn't regress after the token helper rename. Token attached to /echo.
requests.name: OAuth2 client_credentials (regression)

requests.notes: Opens an external browser via shell.openExternal — no in-app HTTP request. Verify by hand: Help → 'Support Rest Hippo…' opens the donation URL in the default browser; no launch-time nag; nothing gated; only https: is passed to shell.openExternal; new strings localized across the 7 locales + native menu.
requests.name: Echo — placeholder
